name: Log Command Redesign

tasks.title: Audit
tasks.description: Verify all work in Log Command Redesign is complete and correct

scope: Implement the wolfcastle log command redesign per spec docs/specs/2026-03-21T18-00Z-log-command-design.md. The spec is the source of truth. Read it before planning. Key goals: (1) wolfcastle log displays daemon activity reconstructed from NDJSON log files in .wolfcastle/system/logs/. (2) Three output modes: summary (default), --thoughts (raw agent output only), --interleaved (stage headers + agent thoughts with timestamps). (3) Default behavior: follow live output if daemon running, replay last session if stopped. (4) Session indexing with --session flag (0=latest, 1=previous, etc.). (5) Flags: --follow/-f, --thoughts/-t, --interleaved/-i, --json, --session/-s. (6) New NDJSON record types (iteration_start, stage_start, stage_complete, audit_report_written, planning_start, planning_complete) should be added to logging package if missing. (7) Non-daemon mode (wolfcastle execute, wolfcastle intake) should stream interleaved format to stdout via a shared renderer. (8) Duration formatting: compact shorthand like 34s, 1m22s, 12m5s, 1h3m. (9) Formatting rules: no sequence numbers, no terminal markers, no log levels. Stage labels: [intake], [plan], [execute], [audit], [remediate], [spec-review]. (10) Exit codes: 0 = success, 1 = no log files found.
success_criteria: wolfcastle log with no flags shows summary output for the most recent session
wolfcastle log --thoughts shows raw agent output with no headers or timestamps
wolfcastle log --interleaved shows stage headers with timestamps, glyphs, and indented agent thoughts
wolfcastle log --json outputs raw NDJSON with no formatting
wolfcastle log --session 1 replays the previous session
wolfcastle log follows live output when daemon is running
wolfcastle execute in non-daemon mode streams interleaved output to stdout
No sequence numbers, terminal markers, or log levels appear in output
Exit code 1 when no log files found, 0 otherwise

breadcrumbs.text: Created 4 children: log-record-types (leaf), log-rendering-engine (orchestrator), log-command-rewrite (leaf), non-daemon-integration (leaf). Ordering: record types first (adds iteration_start needed for session detection), then rendering engine (builds the shared renderers), then command rewrite (consumes the renderers), then non-daemon integration (wires renderers into execute/intake). The rendering engine is an orchestrator because it contains multiple distinct concerns: session detection, duration formatting, and three separate renderers.
